Bash("F:\\skillpal real estate\\.github\\workflows\\deploy.yml" << 'WORKFLOW_EOF'
name: Deploy to Production

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # JOB 1: Build the Next.js frontend on the GitHub Actions runner.
  # We do this here because the runner has more RAM \(7 GB\) than many VPS
  # plans, and it avoids running a memory-hungry Next.js build on the server
  # while live traffic is being served.
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  build-frontend:
    name: Build Next.js Frontend
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Node.js 20
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          cache: 'npm'
          cache-dependency-path: frontend/package-lock.json

      - name: Install frontend dependencies
        working-directory: frontend
        run: npm ci

      - name: Build Next.js app
        working-directory: frontend
        env:
          # Public env vars are baked into the Next.js bundle at build time.
          # Add every NEXT_PUBLIC_* variable your app needs here.
          NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL: ${{ secrets.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL }}
          NEXT_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID: ${{ secrets.NEXT_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID }}
        run: npm run build

      # Upload the compiled .next directory so the deploy job can SCP it.
      - name: Upload .next build artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: nextjs-build
          # .next/cache can be hundreds of MB; exclude it to keep the
          # artifact small. The cache is only useful for rebuilds on the
          # same runner, not on the server.
          path: |
            frontend/.next
            !frontend/.next/cache
          retention-days: 1

  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # JOB 2: Deploy – runs only after the frontend build succeeds.
  # Steps:
  #   a. Download the .next artifact built above.
  #   b. SCP the .next directory to the server.
  #   c. SSH in, pull latest code, build the backend, install frontend
  #      runtime deps, and restart PM2.
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  deploy:
    name: Deploy to Server
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build-frontend   # wait for the build job to finish

    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      # ── Download the .next artifact produced by the build job ──────────
      - name: Download .next build artifact
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: nextjs-build
          # Restore into frontend/ so the directory layout mirrors the repo.
          path: frontend

      # ── SCP: upload the compiled frontend to the server ────────────────
      # We copy only .next \(not node_modules, source files, etc.\) because
      # the server already has the full repo checked out via git pull below.
      - name: Upload .next build to server
        uses: appleboy/scp-action@v0.1.7
        with:
          host: ${{ secrets.HOST }}
          username: ${{ secrets.USERNAME }}
          key: ${{ secrets.KEY }}
          passphrase: ${{ secrets.PASSPHRASE }}
          port: ${{ secrets.PORT }}
          # source path is relative to the GitHub Actions workspace root
          source: "frontend/.next"
          # target is the parent directory on the server; scp will create
          # /var/www/skillpal/frontend/.next automatically
          target: "/var/www/skillpal"
          # IMPORTANT: do NOT set rm: true here. That flag deletes the
          # entire target directory before copying, which would wipe out
          # /var/www/skillpal \(including backend, git repo, etc.\).

      # ── SSH: pull code, build backend, install deps, restart PM2 ───────
      - name: Deploy on Server via SSH
        uses: appleboy/ssh-action@v1.0.3
        env:
          # Pass the PAT as an environment variable so git can authenticate
          # against the private repo without storing credentials on disk.
          GITHUB_PAT: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_PAT }}
        with:
          host: ${{ secrets.HOST }}
          username: ${{ secrets.USERNAME }}
          key: ${{ secrets.KEY }}
          passphrase: ${{ secrets.PASSPHRASE }}
          port: ${{ secrets.PORT }}
          envs: GITHUB_PAT
          # Exit immediately on any error so a failed step is not silently
          # swallowed and PM2 is never restarted with a broken build.
          script_stop: true
          script: |
            set -euo pipefail

            echo "========================================"
            echo " Starting deployment – $\(date -u\)"
            echo "========================================"

            cd /var/www/skillpal

            # ── Git pull ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
            echo "[1/4] Pulling latest code from main..."
            # Inject the PAT into the remote URL so git can reach a private
            # repo without an SSH deploy key configured on the server.
            git remote set-url origin \\
              https://${GITHUB_PAT}@github.com/designdigistratics-dotcom/listing-hub.git
            git fetch origin main
            git reset --hard origin/main

            # ── Backend build ─────────────────────────────────────────────
            echo "[2/4] Building backend..."
            cd /var/www/skillpal/backend

            # npm ci installs exact versions from package-lock.json.
            # We need devDependencies \(TypeScript, ts-node-dev\) to compile,
            # so we do NOT pass --omit=dev here.
            npm ci

            # Regenerate the Prisma client whenever schema.prisma changes.
            npx prisma generate

            # Compile TypeScript to dist/
            npm run build

            # ── Frontend runtime setup ────────────────────────────────────
            # The compiled .next was already uploaded by the SCP step above.
            # We only need to ensure production node_modules are present.
            echo "[3/4] Installing frontend production dependencies..."
            cd /var/www/skillpal/frontend
            npm ci --omit=dev

            # ── Restart PM2 ───────────────────────────────────────────────
            echo "[4/4] Restarting PM2 processes..."
            pm2 restart skillpal-backend
            pm2 restart skillpal-frontend

            # Save the PM2 process list so it survives a server reboot.
            pm2 save

            echo "========================================"
            echo " Deployment complete – $\(date -u\)"
            echo "========================================"
WORKFLOW_EOF)

Bash("F:\\skillpal real estate\\deploy\\deploy.sh" << 'DEPLOY_EOF'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# =============================================================================
# deploy.sh – Production deployment script for Skillpal / ListingHub
#
# Usage \(run as root on the server, or via sudo\):
#   bash /var/www/skillpal/deploy/deploy.sh
#
# The GitHub Actions workflow calls the SSH steps directly and does not
# invoke this script, but it is kept here so you can trigger a manual
# re-deploy from the server without going through GitHub Actions.
# =============================================================================

set -euo pipefail   # exit on error, unset variable, or pipe failure

APP_ROOT="/var/www/skillpal"
LOG_FILE="/var/log/skillpal-deploy.log"
TIMESTAMP="$\(date -u '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S UTC'\)"

# ── Logging helper ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
log\(\) {
  echo "[${TIMESTAMP}] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
}

log "========================================================"
log " Manual deployment started"
log "========================================================"

# ── 1. Pull latest code ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
log "[1/4] Pulling latest code from origin/main..."
cd "$APP_ROOT"

# If you have a GitHub PAT stored in an env file, source it before running
# this script:  source /etc/skillpal/secrets && bash deploy/deploy.sh
if [[ -n "${GITHUB_PAT:-}" ]]; then
  git remote set-url origin \\
    "https://${GITHUB_PAT}@github.com/designdigistratics-dotcom/listing-hub.git"
fi

git fetch origin main
git reset --hard origin/main

# ── 2. Build backend ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
log "[2/4] Building backend \(TypeScript -> dist/\)..."
cd "$APP_ROOT/backend"

# Install all deps including devDependencies \(needed for tsc\)
npm ci

# Regenerate Prisma client in case schema changed
npx prisma generate

# Compile TypeScript
npm run build

# ── 3. Build frontend ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
log "[3/4] Building frontend \(Next.js\)..."
cd "$APP_ROOT/frontend"

# Install all deps \(next build needs them\)
npm ci

# Build the Next.js app.
# NEXT_PUBLIC_* variables must be set in the shell environment before
# running this script if you are doing a full local build on the server.
npm run build

# ── 4. Restart PM2 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
log "[4/4] Restarting PM2 processes..."

pm2 restart skillpal-backend
pm2 restart skillpal-frontend

# Persist the process list so PM2 restores it after a system reboot
pm2 save

log "========================================================"
log " Deployment complete"
log "========================================================"
DEPLOY_EOF)
Bash(ls -la "F:\\skillpal real estate\\deploy"" 2>/dev/null || echo "Directory not found or inaccessible ")
